title: Honda Smart Key System Symptom-by-Symptom Failure Diagnosis Procedure

crossRefs.label: Cannot be locked/unlocked by smart operation (lock/unlock switch) or keyless entry operation
Keyless Entry operation fails to lock/unlock door locks (normal for smart operation)
Door locks cannot be locked/unlocked by smart operation (lock/unlock switch) (normal for keyless entry operation)
Inspection of interior LF antenna and exterior LF antenna
Smart operation does not turn ON mode (backup operation (when smart key contacts ENGINE START STOP switch) also does not turn ON mode)
Smart operation does not turn ON mode, but backup operation (when smart key touches ENGINE START STOP switch) turns ON mode.
Backup operation (when smart key contacts ENGINE START STOP switch) does not turn ON mode, but smart operation does.
Engine does not start (power supply is normal)

blocks.text: Honda Smart Key System Symptom-by-Symptom Failure Diagnosis Procedure
The status of the Honda Smart Key system can be checked with a smart warning light.
The smart warning light illuminates for approximately 2 seconds when the ignition is turned to the ON mode in order for the occupant to confirm that the smart warning light is not illuminated.
Lighting pattern 2 (Fault detection in ignition OFF (LOCK) mode)
If the Honda Smart Key System is determined to be faulty, the smart warning light will illuminate for approximately 3 seconds.
Lighting pattern 3 (fault detection in ignition ON mode)
If the Honda Smart Key system is determined to be faulty, the smart warning light will illuminate until it is restored.
When the B-CAN bus is off, the warning light does not illuminate.

items.text: Check for DTCs before performing functional inspections, and if DTCs are present, diagnose the corresponding DTC failure.
If any abnormality is found during the functional inspection, follow the measures in the table.

rows.text: procedure
(data) item
Inspection method
How to deal with it
Honda smart key
operation check
Verify that the LED on the Honda Smart Key illuminates when the lock/unlock button on the Honda Smart Key is pressed. Or, Honda Smart Key Inspection (1) The company shall
If the LED does not light up, Honda Smart Key Replace Battery Do the following. If replacing the battery does not correct the symptom, replace the Honda Smart Key.
Battery level check

Select "Honda Smart Key ECU" from "One Push Start System" in HDS, then select "System Information Select "Smart Key Battery Voltage" in "System Information" and check the Honda Smart Key battery voltage.
If the battery voltage is less than the specified voltage, Honda Smart Key Replace Battery Do.
Check operation of door lock/unlock switch

1. Select "History Data" of "Honda Smart Key ECU" from "One Push Start System" of HDS, Clear History" is executed. 2. Press the lock/unlock button on each door/tailgate of the Honda Smart Key to lock/unlock. 3. Confirm that the history data for each door, "door lock switch" and "door unlock switch" change from "not confirmed (NG)" to "confirmed (OK)". Confirm that the history data for each door, "door lock switch" and "door unlock switch" change from "unconfirmed (NG)" to "confirmed (OK)".

If it does not work properly, please refer to the symptom-based failure diagnosis " Cannot be locked/unlocked by smart operation (lock/unlock switch) or keyless entry operation ", " Keyless Entry operation fails to lock/unlock door locks (normal for smart operation) and " Door locks cannot be locked/unlocked by smart operation (lock/unlock switch) (normal for keyless entry operation) Perform a failure diagnosis commensurate with one of the symptoms in "1.

rows.text: ENGINE START STOP switch operation check 1 (smart operation)
Hold the Honda Smart Key and verify that the ignition is in the ON mode or that the engine starts.

If the ignition is in the ON mode or the engine will not start, please refer to the symptom-based failure diagnosis " Smart operation does not turn ON mode (backup operation (when smart key contacts ENGINE START STOP switch) also does not turn ON mode) ", " Smart operation does not turn ON mode, but backup operation (when smart key touches ENGINE START STOP switch) turns ON mode. ", " Backup operation (when smart key contacts ENGINE START STOP switch) does not turn ON mode, but smart operation does. ", " Engine does not start (power supply is normal) Perform a failure diagnosis commensurate with one of the symptoms of 。

refs.label: Smart operation does not turn ON mode (backup operation (when smart key contacts ENGINE START STOP switch) also does not turn ON mode)
Smart operation does not turn ON mode, but backup operation (when smart key touches ENGINE START STOP switch) turns ON mode.
Backup operation (when smart key contacts ENGINE START STOP switch) does not turn ON mode, but smart operation does.
Engine does not start (power supply is normal)

rows.text: ENGINE START STOP switch operation check 2 (backup operation)

1. Remove the battery from the Honda Smart Key. 2. Retain the Honda Smart Key and enter the vehicle. 3. Press the ENGINE START STOP switch and confirm that the buzzer sounds and the ENGINE START STOP switch indicator lights. 4. When the Honda Smart Key has been authenticated, confirm that the ENGINE START STOP switch indicator changes from flashing to lit and the buzzer blows once. 5. Ensure that the ignition is in the ignition ON mode. If the brake pedal is not depressed, press the ENGINE START STOP switch once for ACC mode and twice for ON mode.

blocks.text: The status of the Honda Smart Key system can be checked via the immobilizer indicator.

If immobilizer verification is performed correctly when the ignition is in the ignition ON mode, the immobilizer indicator lights up after the ignition is in the ignition ON mode and turns off after verification is complete.

If the immobilizer is not properly matched when the ignition is in the ON mode, the immobilizer indicator will illuminate and then flash after the ignition is in the ON mode. After the ignition is turned OFF (LOCK) mode, the indicator will blink for 10 seconds and then turn off. the registration status of the Honda Smart Key and the status of the S-NET line can be checked by checking the system check.

Before inspecting the Honda Smart Key System, the following items should be checked and, if applicable, the following actions should be taken. In addition, an interview shall be conducted to determine the circumstances of the non-operation of the system.

items.text: Inspect No. A4-1 (60A), No. A4-2 (50A), and No. A10 (10A) fuses in the relay box for blown fuses and connections.
Inspect No.C7 (7.5A), No.C11 (10A), No.C19 (15A) and No.C29 (7.5A) fuses in the fuse box for blown fuses and connections.
Check the connection of the coupler of the electronic steering lock.
Check the coupler connection of the ENGINE START STOP switch.
If equipped with a HAC remote control engine starter, check for proper installation.
Check the connections of each coupler in the fuse box.
Check for abnormalities in the DC-DC converter (check the DTC of the PGM-FI system).
Perform ALL DTC CHECK in HDS, and if a DTC is displayed, diagnose the corresponding DTC failure.
Make sure the Honda Smart Key is not touching or covered by metal.
Ensure that the vehicle's 12V battery is sufficiently charged.
Inspect the battery in the Honda Smart Key and replace if necessary.
Make sure it is not stored with another Honda Smart Key.
Make sure the Honda Smart Key is not stored in a purse or other container with coins or other metal objects.
If the vehicle is equipped with equipment other than genuine equipment, deactivate the equipment.
If electrical components are connected to the S-NET line, remove the connected electrical components.
If a remote engine starter is installed, check that it is properly connected.
If there are facilities emitting strong radio waves in the vicinity, move to a new location.
Check to see that they are not being carried with communication devices such as cell phones, radios, or laptops.
If the failure symptoms are not reproduced, check the HDS status log and system check, and diagnose the failure according to the table.
When checking the status log, check the number of log counts and diagnose failures in the order of the number of detections.
The HDS menu is divided into the Honda Smart Key ECU and the Backup Control Unit, so check the system information for each.
Although the HDS has a separate menu screen, the Honda Smart Key Control Unit and Backup Control Unit are built into the MICU.
